title: Bihai purpurea
intro: Bihai purpurea, also known as Purple Perilla, is an annual plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to Asia, characterized by its edible leaves and purple flowers. The health benefits of Bihai purpurea include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of allergies, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the most common preparations made with Bihai purpurea include teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being used as a flavoring agent in various foods and beverages.
taxonomy: Bihai purpurea is classified as a species within the genus Bihai and the family Bihaiaceae, which is a part of the larger order Lamiales. The exact classification may vary based on different botanical sources. The species Bihai purpurea is a type of flowering plant. Bihai purpurea is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of the plant.
